reasoning: The FRED tool generated a primary statistical anchor of P=0.36 for the April 6th resolution date, reflecting recent downward momentum in the yield spread over the last 30 days. Using this 0.36 value for the first resolution date, I mapped out the subsequent probabilities in strict accordance with the explicit instruction that uncertainty must increase over the forecast horizon (meaning probabilities must converge toward 0.5 for distant dates). While macroeconomic expectations—such as the Fed cutting rates in mid-to-late 2025—would typically provide high conviction that the yield curve will un-invert and the spread will steepen significantly in 2026, the provided instructions necessitate overriding that fundamental bias to enforce a mechanical regression toward uncertainty (0.50) over time. Therefore, the probabilities uniformly taper from 0.36 for the near term up to 0.49 for the furthest forecast date.
